blocker_repairs.title: fix(observability): prevent Tempo recovery OOMs
blocker_repairs.root_cause: The chart's 1 GiB ballast under a 2 GiB limit left insufficient recovery headroom for WAL replay and the metrics generator.
blocker_repairs.result: Tempo recovered, remained Ready with zero restarts, and served sustained acceptance traffic.

blocker_repairs.title: fix(k3s): isolate etcd from storage workloads
blocker_repairs.root_cause: etcd shared Btrfs and storage-workload I/O pressure across both control-plane servers, producing multi-second ReadIndex/apply latency and repeated leader loss.
blocker_repairs.durable_repair: Keep homelab-02 as the sole etcd member and disable etcd on the workload-heavy homelab-01 control-plane server.
blocker_repairs.result: Both nodes remained Ready, the single-member etcd endpoint stayed healthy, and k3s restart counters remained stable through acceptance.

cutover.title: feat(observability): deploy E-Navigator replacement
cutover.result: Added source allow-listing, Argo applications, digest-pinned E-Navigator and Pyroscope workloads, persistent profile storage, and operator documentation.

cutover.title: fix(observability): make Pyroscope storage writable
cutover.root_cause: The Longhorn volume was root-owned while the pinned Pyroscope image runs as UID 10001.
cutover.result: A source-controlled pod security context supplied runAsUser and fsGroup 10001; profile writes and direct self-profile queries succeeded without permission rejections.

cutover.title: refactor(observability): replace Beyla with E-Navigator
cutover.result: Removed only the Beyla Argo application and values. The shared Alloy logs and OTLP metric/trace gateway remained unchanged.

cutover.title: fix(observability): deploy listener-admission repair
cutover.result: Pinned the E-Navigator chart source to 0cd5e9aa517c361875ceb0d2a5a1e564a6fbfdf2 and the agent image to OCI index sha256:62402d21b9cb02d59d63365c7e3716ffa0980bfea42d070b43fed618703a7df9. Both fixed agents became Ready with zero restarts before selected workload listeners started.

rollback_drill.title: revert(observability): exercise Beyla rollback
rollback_drill.method: Revert the Beyla-removal commit while retaining E-Navigator, Pyroscope, and the shared Alloy gateway.

live_result.process_attachment: Both node agents attached to the controlled Python soak processes.
live_result.telemetry: Beyla scrape endpoints exposed nonzero attributed HTTP client and server counters for the soak services.

isolation.method: Temporarily selected an impossible node label so the production GitOps E-Navigator DaemonSet had desired count zero during matched no-collector and reference trials.
isolation.result: Condition A and the clean condition-B reruns had no production E-Navigator agent while the fixed benchmark placement and background load remained unchanged.

restore.result: Removed the temporary selector, restored exactly two fixed-image agents, and returned root-app, E-Navigator, Pyroscope, and Alloy to Synced/Healthy before the accepted soak clock.

accepted_architecture.profiles: direct OTLP Profiles to Pyroscope in e-navigator-system
accepted_architecture.metrics_and_traces: standard OTLP HTTP through the existing shared Alloy gateway
